searchLedger.hypothesis: Simultaneous first acquisition without an existing owner lease is guarded

searchActions.result: The no-existing-lease path uses fs.openSync(..., 'wx') for exclusive creation (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:339-346); losers read the holder after EEXIST (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:431-438). The launcher also rechecks owner lease ownership before writing the PID guard and before spawning (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:978-982, 866-870).

searchLedger.rationale: Two launchers starting from no owner lease cannot both atomically create .skill-advisor-owner.json. A loser either bridges/reports or, if ownership changes before spawn, launchServer refuses to spawn.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: O_CREAT|O_EXCL-style acquisition plus owner reprobes prevent the no-prior-lease double-writer interleaving.

title: Stale launcher lease ignores recorded live childPid before spawning a replacement
evidence: writeLeaseFile records childPid when the advisor child is spawned (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:595-607, 903), but stale reclaim only checks lease.pid, marks the lease stale, logs it, and falls through (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:558-587). The spec-memory reference explicitly reads staleLease.childPid, adopts a live bridgeable daemon, or takes a respawn lock and reaps before respawn (.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:1496-1540).
rationale: Interleaving: launcher A writes a lease with pid=A and childPid=D, then A dies or loses heartbeat while D remains alive/bridgeable but not represented by a live daemon lease yet, or the built daemon lease module is unavailable/stale. Launcher B reclaims the owner lease, leaseHeldFromFile checks only A and returns stale, the daemon-lease fallback does not prove D live, then B writes pid=B and spawns D2. D and D2 can now both write the same daemon state. The recorded childPid was the durable handle needed to adopt or safely reap D; the port does neither.

title: Bridge respawn decisions are returned but never acted on
evidence: bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld returns maybeBridgeLeaseHolder's decision directly (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:537-546), and checkStrictSingleWriter treats every awaited bridge/report call as terminal success (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:553-554, 561-562, 575-576). The reference handles decision.action === 'respawn' by entering respawnAfterDeadSocket under bootstrap/respawn locks (.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:839-856, 756-837).
rationale: If a held owner/daemon lease has a socket path but deep probing declares the socket dead, the shared bridge contract can return action:'respawn'. The skill-advisor launcher drops that decision and exits, so no launcher adopts, reaps, or respawns. Repeated fresh sessions will report/exit instead of recovering until an external cleanup occurs.
